entries.title: silicon-metatrader5: MetaTrader 5 Solution for macOS Silicon M Series
entries.summary: End-to-end solution for MT5 on Apple Silicon using Docker + Wine + QEMU x86_64 emulation. Provides custom Python client 'siliconmetatrader5' that bypasses native MetaTrader5 library limitations. Uses Colima for x86_64 emulation, avoiding Rosetta instability.

provenance.query: Python trading bot MetaTrader 5 macOS Apple Silicon bridge

entries.title: MetaTrader 5 Python Integration: 2026 Best Practices
entries.summary: ZeroMQ architecture with dual socket patterns: PUB/SUB for market data, REQ/REP for order execution. MQL5 EA acts as ZMQ server with C++ DLL wrapper. Heartbeat protocol every 5s with 500ms timeout for connection health.

provenance.query: Python trading bot MetaTrader 5 macOS Apple Silicon bridge

entries.title: MT5RemoteBridgeAPI - Production-grade MT5 ZeroMQ Bridge
entries.summary: Military-grade security using ZAP + Curve25519 encryption. Microsecond latency, async messaging. Full Python client with test suite. Requires Allow DLL imports in MT5.

provenance.query: Python trading bot MetaTrader 5 macOS Apple Silicon bridge

entries.title: mt5-mac-data-bridge: JSON File IPC Bridge
entries.summary: macOS-native bridge using local JSON file IPC instead of sockets. EA writes price/tick/orderbook JSON files; Python reads and writes commands.json. No Wine, no Docker required. Production-friendly with structured logs.

provenance.query: Python trading bot MetaTrader 5 macOS Apple Silicon bridge

entries.title: Building a Trading Gateway with MT5 Python Library
entries.summary: Trading gateway architecture using official MetaTrader5 Python package as relay. Components: strategy, indicator updater, trading gateway, message queue (ZeroMQ/RabbitMQ/Kafka). Requires MT5 terminal running in foreground.

provenance.query: Python trading bot MetaTrader 5 macOS Apple Silicon bridge

entries.title: mql-zmq: Complete ZeroMQ binding for MQL4/5
entries.summary: Complete ZMQ binding for MQL4/5 language. Enables traders to build cross-platform communication between MT4/5 and external applications.

entries.summary: Official MetaTrader5 package only provides Windows distributions (win_amd64 wheels). Native installation on macOS fails - no macOS version available.

entries.title: Battle-Tested Backtesters: VectorBT, Zipline, Backtrader Comparison
entries.summary: VectorBT: vectorized, Numba JIT, millions of trades in under a second. Zipline: event-driven, pandas backend, slow on large datasets. Backtrader: flexible, stable, connects to real markets. VectorBT undisputed leader for speed.

provenance.query: vectorbt backtrader zipline backtesting framework Python ARM Apple Silicon

entries.title: Backtrader vs Zipline: 5-Year S&P 500 Speed & Memory Test
entries.summary: M1 MacBook Pro benchmark: Zipline 4.2s vs Backtrader 15.6s, but Zipline peaks at 1.8GB RAM vs Backtrader's 600MB. At 20-year backtest: Zipline 14.3s/5.8GB vs Backtrader 54.1s/641MB. Crossover at 6-8 indicators.

provenance.query: vectorbt backtrader zipline backtesting framework Python ARM Apple Silicon

entries.title: RaptorBT: Rust backtesting engine with Python bindings
entries.summary: Rust + PyO3, drop-in VectorBT replacement. Apple Silicon benchmarks: 5800x faster than VectorBT on 1K bars (0.25ms vs 1460ms), 25x faster on 50K bars. 45x smaller disk footprint (<10MB vs 450MB).

provenance.query: vectorbt backtrader zipline backtesting framework Python ARM Apple Silicon

entries.title: Backtrader vs VectorBT: Python Trading Framework Comparison
entries.summary: VectorBT processes 1M orders in 70-100ms on M1. 1000 stocks × 10 years in under 1 minute. VectorBT: low memory (vectorized), high CPU (parallel). Backtrader: moderate (sequential, single-threaded).

provenance.query: vectorbt backtrader zipline backtesting framework Python ARM Apple Silicon

entries.title: VectorBT Apple Silicon M1 Installation
entries.summary: Installation requires Miniforge/Conda for Numba on ARM. 2025 solution: pyenv + Python 3.10 + poetry install vectorbt works on M2. Conda required for llvmlite/numba ARM wheels.

entries.title: Backtrader vs NautilusTrader vs VectorBT vs Zipline-reloaded
entries.summary: NautilusTrader: Rust core, unified backtest+live, native risk controls. VectorBT: research powerhouse, zero live trading. Backtrader: flexible but slow at scale. Research-only: VectorBT/Zipline. Production: NautilusTrader.

entries.title: Creating a Self-Improving Trading Bot with Reinforcement Learning
entries.summary: RL trading: agent observes state, takes action (buy/sell/hold), receives reward, updates policy, repeats. PPO recommended (balances exploration/exploitation). Self-improvement via: online learning, retraining pipelines, adaptive reward functions.

provenance.query: self-improving autonomous trading bot architecture Bayesian optimization evolutionary algorithm

entries.title: Multi-Agent RL + Evolutionary + Bayesian Optimization
entries.summary: Two-layer architecture: Layer 1 = DRL agents with diverse reward functions. Layer 2 = EA/BO on Layer 1 outputs. BO builds Gaussian Process surrogate, uses acquisition function (EI, UCB) for exploration/exploitation. EA produces sparse allocations, BO produces smoother.

provenance.query: self-improving autonomous trading bot architecture Bayesian optimization evolutionary algorithm

entries.title: ETARE: Evolutionary Trading Algorithm with Reinforcement Learning
entries.summary: LSTM-based system with extinction mechanism: every 100 trades, removes unprofitable strategies. Adaptive learning with prioritized memory. Continuous trading loop with periodic strategy cleaning. PyTorch multi-layered backpropagation.

provenance.query: self-improving autonomous trading bot architecture Bayesian optimization evolutionary algorithm

entries.title: Population Based Training for Trading (DeepMind PBT)
entries.summary: PBT combines random search + evolutionary methods in single training process. Poorly performing agents inherit weights/hyperparameters of better agents while exploring perturbations. Asynchronous operation. Fitness metric: Sharpe ratio or PnL.

provenance.query: self-improving autonomous trading bot architecture Bayesian optimization evolutionary algorithm

entries.title: Bayesian vs Evolutionary Optimization for Crypto Trading
entries.summary: TPE (via Optuna) for Bayesian optimization: models p(x|y), separates good/poor performance groups. DE (scipy) maintains population with mutation/crossover/selection. RL adaptive but computationally prohibitive for backtesting.

provenance.query: self-improving autonomous trading bot architecture Bayesian optimization evolutionary algorithm

entries.title: Portfolio Risk Management for Automated Strategies
entries.summary: Three-layer framework: trade-level, strategy-level, portfolio-level limits. Drawdown limits non-negotiable: daily loss, trailing drawdown stop, max strategy loss. Most important: portfolio-level drawdown + position sizing combined.

provenance.query: algorithmic trading risk management framework Kelly criterion position sizing drawdown control

entries.title: Risk Management Using Kelly Criterion
entries.summary: Fractional Kelly (25-50% of full recommendation) significantly reduces volatility while capturing most growth. Full Kelly leads to 20-50% drawdowns. Recalculate daily (active) or weekly. Update Kelly % every 3-6 months with rolling lookback.

provenance.query: algorithmic trading risk management framework Kelly criterion position sizing drawdown control

entries.title: Drawdown Management in Algorithmic Trading
entries.summary: Max drawdown 20-25% before auto-pause. Position scaling: at 10% DD reduce 30%, at 15% reduce 50%, at 20% stop entirely. Circuit breakers prevent compounding losses. Strategy abandonment at DD bottom is the worst timing.

provenance.query: algorithmic trading risk management framework Kelly criterion position sizing drawdown control

entries.title: Risk Engine: Dynamic Circuit Breakers
entries.summary: Static thresholds fail in dynamic markets. Adaptive circuit breakers with dynamic window size based on volatility. Online meta-learning model for higher-order pattern detection. Balance false positives vs false negatives.

provenance.query: algorithmic trading risk management framework Kelly criterion position sizing drawdown control

entries.title: Risk Engine: Stochastic Position Sizing
entries.summary: StochasticPositionSizer: min/max contracts, drawdown_sensitivity (aggression of reduction), profit_smoothing (recent profit influence), recovery_rate (scaling back up). Dynamic sizing treats position size as a process, not formula.

provenance.query: algorithmic trading risk management framework Kelly criterion position sizing drawdown control

entries.title: Why Expected Value Is Not Enough in Production Trading
entries.summary: Fractional Kelly (25% as hard constraint) + CVaR optimization (worst 5% scenarios). Track CVaR daily, not just PnL. Assume 30% parameter uncertainty. Kill-switches for anomalies. Explicit 'model is completely wrong' code paths.

provenance.query: algorithmic trading risk management framework Kelly criterion position sizing drawdown control

entries.title: Python in High-Frequency Trading: Low-Latency Techniques
entries.summary: Asyncio for event loop, async I/O while waiting for network/disk. GIL limitation mitigated by multiprocessing or async. Multiprocessing for embarrassingly parallel tasks despite IPC overhead.

provenance.query: Python trading bot memory optimization Apple Silicon unified memory asyncio multiprocessing

entries.title: 2026 Mac GPU AI Inference: M5 Max Unified Memory Review
entries.summary: M5 Max unified memory eliminates VRAM bottleneck. Model weights should stay under 60% of total unified memory for macOS + KV cache + runtime headroom. MLX native on Apple Silicon in 2026.

entries.title: Modular Python Backtesting Framework with Threading + Multiprocessing
entries.summary: Threading for I/O-bound speedups (CSV loading). Multiprocessing for parameter optimization (CPU-bound). Calculation-intensive backtesting prefers multiprocessing over multithreading.
